title: Smartphone How-To and Troubleshooting
description: Practical smartphone operation and troubleshooting guides.
text: smartphone phone how to search troubleshooting wifi bluetooth charging restart reset backup

title: Smartphone Touch Gestures: Tap, Long-Press, Swipe and Pinch
description: Learn tap, long-press, swipe, pinch and drag gestures, including practical ways to avoid accidental actions.
text: Smartphone Touch Gestures: Tap, Long-Press, Swipe and Pinch Learn tap, long-press, swipe, pinch and drag gestures, including practical ways to avoid accidental actions. Check that the screen is dry and the protector is not lifting Use the pad of your finger rather than a fingernail Test another area or app to see whether the issue is general Tap once lightly; pressing harder normally does not help. Long-press the same spot for about a second, then release when a menu or edit mode appears. Swipe while keeping contact with the screen to move a list or page. Spread two fingers to zoom in and pinch them together to zoom out. To drag, long-press an item and move it without lifting your finger.

title: How to Copy and Paste Text on a Smartphone
description: Copy, cut and paste text on iPhone and Android, including how to adjust a selection and what to do when text cannot be selected.
text: How to Copy and Paste Text on a Smartphone Copy, cut and paste text on iPhone and Android, including how to adjust a selection and what to do when text cannot be selected. Do not leave passwords or card numbers in the clipboard Check whether the apparent text is actually part of an image Some apps intentionally prevent copying Long-press near the text you want. Drag the selection handles until the correct range is highlighted. Choose Copy from the menu. Open the destination, long-press the text field and choose Paste. Use Cut only when you intend to remove the original text.

title: How to Take a Screenshot on iPhone and Android
description: Take screenshots with buttons or on-screen controls, capture long pages where supported, and troubleshoot blocked captures.
text: How to Take a Screenshot on iPhone and Android Take screenshots with buttons or on-screen controls, capture long pages where supported, and troubleshoot blocked captures. Check for personal data, notifications or location information Streaming, financial and corporate apps may block screenshots Crop unnecessary areas before sharing On Face ID iPhones, briefly press the side and volume-up buttons together. On Home-button models, press the side or top button with Home. On many Android phones, briefly press power and volume down together; some models also provide a shortcut in Quick Settings. Open the preview to crop or annotate the image. Use Full Page or Scrolling Capture only when the app and device offer it. If capture fails, avoid holding the buttons too long and check whether the app blocks screenshots.

title: How to Scan a QR Code Safely on a Smartphone
description: Scan QR codes with the camera or scanner, inspect the destination and handle codes that will not open.
text: How to Scan a QR Code Safely on a Smartphone Scan QR codes with the camera or scanner, inspect the destination and handle codes that will not open. Fit the whole code in view with adequate light Read the displayed domain before opening it For payments or sign-in, reopen the service through its official app Open the standard camera and frame the entire code. Before tapping, inspect the domain and whether the destination matches the expected purpose. Change the angle or distance if printed material reflects light. Increase the other screen’s brightness when scanning a displayed code. For a saved image, use the photo app’s recognition feature or Google Lens where available.

title: How to Search Google Effectively on a Smartphone
description: Build better search queries, recognize official sources, and narrow results by date, place and purpose.
text: How to Search Google Effectively on a Smartphone Build better search queries, recognize official sources, and narrow results by date, place and purpose. Separate a fact, an action and a symptom Include the device model or operating system Distinguish sponsored results from organic results Enter a specific subject and goal, such as “Pixel not charging” or “iPhone Wi-Fi settings.” Put a distinctive error message in quotation marks to find exact matches. Prefer the manufacturer or service’s official site for prices, specifications and procedures. Add an OS version or year when old instructions appear. When sources disagree, compare model, country and publication date.

title: How to Use Voice Search on a Smartphone
description: Use voice search and troubleshoot microphone permissions, language settings and recognition problems.
text: How to Use Voice Search on a Smartphone Use voice search and troubleshoot microphone permissions, language settings and recognition problems. Do not speak sensitive information in public Check microphone permission for the search app Make sure the selected language matches what you speak Tap the microphone in the Google app, browser or search widget. Speak a short phrase and separate names or places clearly. Correct the text or repeat the request with more detail if recognition is wrong. If nothing happens, test the microphone with a recording app. If recording works, check the search app’s microphone permission, language and connection.

title: How to Search with Google Lens on a Smartphone
description: Use Google Lens with a camera or saved photo to identify objects, places and text while reducing false matches.
text: How to Search with Google Lens on a Smartphone Use Google Lens with a camera or saved photo to identify objects, places and text while reducing false matches. Avoid uploading faces, addresses or other unnecessary personal details Verify product names and model numbers with official information Do not make medical or safety decisions from image search alone Open Lens from the Google app, Google Photos, Chrome or another supported entry point. Take a photo or choose an existing one, then crop to the relevant area. For a small object, use good light, move closer and reduce background clutter. Choose Text to copy or translate visible writing. When several matches appear, compare shape, logo, model number and location.

title: How to Find Text on a Web Page in Chrome and Safari
description: Use Find in Page in Chrome and Safari to locate a word or phrase within a long page.
text: How to Find Text on a Web Page in Chrome and Safari Use Find in Page in Chrome and Safari to locate a word or phrase within a long page. Use Find in Page rather than the normal web-search box Try spelling, spacing or character-width variations Make sure the page has fully loaded In Chrome, open the menu and choose Find in page. In Safari, open the page or Share menu and choose Find on Page. Enter a short term and use the match counter and arrows to move between results. If nothing matches, try part of the word, a different spelling or just the number. For a PDF, use the search function provided by the PDF viewer.

title: Phone Not Connecting to Wi-Fi: iPhone and Android Fixes
description: Troubleshoot missing networks, password errors and “connected but no internet” problems on iPhone and Android.
text: Phone Not Connecting to Wi-Fi: iPhone and Android Fixes Troubleshoot missing networks, password errors and “connected but no internet” problems on iPhone and Android. Check whether other devices can use the same network Make sure Airplane Mode is not enabled On public or managed Wi-Fi, look for a sign-in or terms page Turn Wi-Fi off, wait about ten seconds and turn it on again. Select the network and re-enter the password, checking capitalization, similar characters and trailing spaces. If connected without internet, open a browser and look for a hotel or public-network sign-in page. Restart the phone and router; check with the administrator before unplugging managed equipment. If only one network fails, forget it and connect again. Consider resetting network settings only after simpler steps; saved Wi-Fi and Bluetooth settings may be removed.

title: Mobile Data Not Working: A Step-by-Step Phone Checklist
description: Troubleshoot no service, unusable data despite signal bars, and SIM or eSIM issues.
text: Mobile Data Not Working: A Step-by-Step Phone Checklist Troubleshoot no service, unusable data despite signal bars, and SIM or eSIM issues. Check data allowance, account status and carrier outages Make sure mobile data is enabled for the correct SIM When abroad, verify roaming settings and country coverage Turn Airplane Mode on for about ten seconds, then off, to reconnect to the network. Check that mobile data is enabled and that the intended line is selected on a dual-SIM device. Restart the phone normally. Check carrier outage notices, maintenance, data limits and account restrictions. After a SIM change, verify activation, APN and eSIM status using the carrier’s official instructions. If the issue remains, consider a network-settings reset or contact the carrier.

title: Bluetooth Device Will Not Connect to a Phone: Fixes
description: Troubleshoot earbuds, cars, keyboards and other Bluetooth devices that cannot be found or connected.
text: Bluetooth Device Will Not Connect to a Phone: Fixes Troubleshoot earbuds, cars, keyboards and other Bluetooth devices that cannot be found or connected. Put the accessory in pairing mode Check whether it is already connected to another device Make sure it is charged and within range Turn Bluetooth off, wait a few seconds and turn it on again. Put the accessory into pairing mode using its manufacturer instructions. If it was paired before, select its name and try connecting. If that fails, forget the device on the phone and clear the accessory’s pairing history before pairing again. Restart both the phone and accessory. Consider a network-settings reset only if several Bluetooth devices fail similarly.

title: Phone Slow or Frozen: What to Try Before a Factory Reset
description: Diagnose slow performance, freezes and unexpected restarts through restart, storage and app checks before resetting.
text: Phone Slow or Frozen: What to Try Before a Factory Reset Diagnose slow performance, freezes and unexpected restarts through restart, storage and app checks before resetting. If the phone is hot, stop charging or intensive use and let it cool Check whether storage is nearly full Identify apps installed or updated shortly before the problem Use a normal restart if the screen responds; otherwise use the model-specific force-restart procedure. Check for operating-system and app updates. Review free storage and remove unnecessary videos, duplicate files and offline content. Android notes that issues may begin when less than 10% of storage is free. If one app triggers the freeze, close, update, clear its temporary data where appropriate, then reinstall if needed. On supported Android devices, Safe Mode can help identify a third-party app problem. If nothing helps, back up before a factory reset and contact the manufacturer when hardware failure is possible.

title: App Keeps Crashing or Will Not Open on a Phone
description: Fix an app that will not open or keeps closing by checking updates, permissions, storage and reinstall options.
text: App Keeps Crashing or Will Not Open on a Phone Fix an app that will not open or keeps closing by checking updates, permissions, storage and reinstall options. Check whether the service itself is experiencing an outage Determine whether important data exists only inside the app Make sure you can sign in and complete two-step verification Force-close the app and open it again. Restart the phone. Check for an app update in the official store and for an OS update. Review free storage and required permissions such as camera, photos or microphone. On Android, clear cache using official steps where appropriate; clearing app data is different and may remove local information. Before reinstalling, confirm account sync, backups and any transfer code.

title: Phone Storage Full: How to Free Space Safely
description: Free phone storage safely by reviewing photos, videos, apps, downloads and offline files without losing needed data.
text: Phone Storage Full: How to Free Space Safely Free phone storage safely by reviewing photos, videos, apps, downloads and offline files without losing needed data. Verify backup or cloud sync is complete Check whether deleting from the device also deletes the cloud copy Include message attachments and downloads in the review Open the Storage settings and identify the largest categories. Start with unnecessary videos, screen recordings, bursts and duplicates; Recently Deleted may still use space. Before removing an unused app, confirm how its local data and account recovery work. Review downloaded music, video, maps and podcasts. Check browser downloads and large message attachments. When using cloud optimization, remember that originals may need to be downloaded again later.

title: Phone Battery Draining Fast: Checks and Fixes
description: Identify display, network, background-app and battery-health causes of rapid battery drain.
text: Phone Battery Draining Fast: Checks and Fixes Identify display, network, background-app and battery-health causes of rapid battery drain. Review battery usage by app Consider heat, cold and time spent in weak-signal areas After setup or a major update, allow for temporary background processing Restart the phone and install OS and app updates. Use Battery settings to compare screen and app consumption. Adjust brightness, auto-lock, always-on display, location and background activity as appropriate. Weak signal increases radio use, so use stable Wi-Fi or disable unused connections when practical. Enable battery-saver mode and compare the result. If battery health or diagnostics show significant degradation, contact the manufacturer or an authorized repair provider.

title: Phone Not Charging: Cable, Port and Device Checks
description: Troubleshoot no charging, slow charging and interrupted charging by checking power, cables, ports and temperature.
text: Phone Not Charging: Cable, Port and Device Checks Troubleshoot no charging, slow charging and interrupted charging by checking power, cables, ports and temperature. Stop use if the device is wet, smells burnt or is swollen Compare with another safe cable, adapter and outlet Never insert metal objects into the charging port Inspect the cable, adapter and outlet; stop using damaged or discolored equipment. Try another compatible charger and power source, changing one component at a time. If debris is visible, power off and follow manufacturer cleaning guidance; do not use a pin or metal tool. Let an excessively hot or cold device return to a normal temperature before charging. After a fully depleted battery, leave it connected to a supported charger for a while before trying to start it. For wireless charging, check case thickness, alignment and foreign objects; seek service if the issue remains.

title: How to Force Restart an iPhone by Model
description: Force restart an unresponsive iPhone using the correct button sequence for Face ID, iPhone 7 and older models.
text: How to Force Restart an iPhone by Model Force restart an unresponsive iPhone using the correct button sequence for Face ID, iPhone 7 and older models. A force restart is not a factory reset Use a normal restart when the phone still responds If the battery may be empty, connect a safe charger For iPhone 8 and later, including Face ID models, quickly press and release volume up, then quickly press and release volume down. Hold the side button until the Apple logo appears. Continue holding if the power-off slider appears, then release at the logo. On iPhone 7 or 7 Plus, hold the side and volume-down buttons together until the Apple logo appears. On iPhone 6s and earlier, hold the Home and side or top buttons until the Apple logo appears. If it will not start, charge for about an hour and then continue with display, charging or service checks.

title: How to Force Restart an Android Phone
description: Force restart a frozen Android phone and account for manufacturer-specific button combinations safely.
text: How to Force Restart an Android Phone Force restart a frozen Android phone and account for manufacturer-specific button combinations safely. A force restart is normally different from a data-erasing factory reset Button combinations vary by manufacturer and model If a boot menu appears, do not select an erase option First hold the power button for about 30 seconds and see whether the device restarts. If it does not, check the manufacturer’s official model-specific procedure. Many phones use power plus volume down, but timing and behavior vary. Release the buttons when the logo appears and allow startup to finish. If a Recovery or Bootloader screen appears, do not choose Factory reset or Wipe data; choose Restart or Reboot system now. For repeated freezes, review storage, updates, problematic apps and overheating.

title: How to Reset Network Settings on a Smartphone
description: Understand what a network-settings reset removes and how to prepare before using it for Wi-Fi, Bluetooth or mobile-data problems.
text: How to Reset Network Settings on a Smartphone Understand what a network-settings reset removes and how to prepare before using it for Wi-Fi, Bluetooth or mobile-data problems. Know the passwords for Wi-Fi networks you need Know how to pair important Bluetooth accessories again Record VPN, APN, dual-SIM and related settings Try lower-impact steps first, including restart, Airplane Mode and forgetting only the affected Wi-Fi network. On iPhone, go to Settings > General > Transfer or Reset iPhone > Reset > Reset Network Settings; wording can vary by OS version. On Android, use Settings > System > Reset options and choose the Wi-Fi, mobile and Bluetooth reset; the path varies by manufacturer. Afterward, reconnect Wi-Fi and verify Bluetooth devices, VPN and cellular service one at a time. For managed phones or custom APN configurations, consult the administrator or carrier first.

title: Complete Smartphone Backup Checklist Before a Factory Reset
description: Check photos, contacts, messages, authenticator apps, eSIM and app-specific data before erasing a phone.
text: Complete Smartphone Backup Checklist Before a Factory Reset Check photos, contacts, messages, authenticator apps, eSIM and app-specific data before erasing a phone. Verify that the backup can be restored, not merely that one exists Confirm Apple or Google account credentials Check separate transfer steps for authenticators, banking, payment and messaging apps Open recent photos and videos from iCloud, Google Photos, a computer or another device to verify sync. Check the sync destination and last update for contacts, calendars, notes and messages. Create a full device backup and record its date, size and encryption status. Complete separate transfer procedures for authenticator, password-manager, banking, payment, messaging and game apps. Ask the carrier whether the eSIM can remain during reset and how it would be reissued if removed. Confirm Apple or Google account passwords, screen lock and recovery methods. Before erasing, open files from the backup destination to prove they are accessible.

title: How to Factory Reset an iPhone Safely
description: Erase all content and settings on an iPhone safely after checking backup, eSIM and account access.
text: How to Factory Reset an iPhone Safely Erase all content and settings on an iPhone safely after checking backup, eSIM and account access. This action erases personal data, apps and settings from the device Verify that a backup can be restored The eSIM choice differs for resale versus reuse by the same person Confirm the reason for erasing; for troubleshooting, try restart, updates, storage cleanup and targeted settings resets first. Complete the backup checklist and confirm Apple Account access and recovery methods. Open Settings > General > Transfer or Reset iPhone > Erase All Content and Settings. Read the erase summary and enter the device passcode or Apple Account password when requested. Choose whether to keep or erase the eSIM based on reuse, transfer and carrier reissue rules. Keep the phone powered until erasure finishes; restore a backup for reuse, or stop at the setup screen before transfer.

title: How to Factory Reset an Android Phone Safely
description: Prepare account access and backups before erasing an Android phone through Settings.
text: How to Factory Reset an Android Phone Safely Prepare account access and backups before erasing an Android phone through Settings. A factory reset erases data stored on the phone Confirm the Google Account email and password After a recent password change, check official waiting guidance For troubleshooting, first try restart, updates, storage cleanup, app checks and a targeted network reset. Confirm Google Account access, screen lock, backups, authenticator transfer, eSIM and app-specific migration. On many devices, open Settings > System > Reset options > Erase all data; labels vary by manufacturer. Read the erase summary and enter the PIN, pattern or password. Charge the phone sufficiently and do not interrupt the erase process. After reset, previous Google Account verification may be required. Before transfer, follow the manufacturer’s official account-removal procedure.

title: Lost Phone: Find, Lock and Erase It Remotely
description: Locate, lock and if necessary erase a lost iPhone or Android phone while protecting accounts and carrier service.
text: Lost Phone: Find, Lock and Erase It Remotely Locate, lock and if necessary erase a lost iPhone or Android phone while protecting accounts and carrier service. Do not enter a dangerous location to recover the phone yourself Remote erasure may limit or stop further location tracking Provide necessary details to police, venues and the carrier Use another device to open Apple Find My or Google Find Hub from an official URL, not an advertisement or third-party tracker. Review location, last-seen time and battery state; play a sound only when it is safe and useful. Mark the device as lost or secure it, and display a contact message without revealing unlock information. Contact the carrier about suspending or replacing the SIM or eSIM and handling the device identifier. Report the loss to police or the venue and record time, place, device details and tracking evidence. When recovery is unlikely and data sensitivity is high, consider remote erasure only after checking how it affects tracking and account protection. Review active sessions, passwords and two-step verification from a trusted device.

title: How to Recognize Phishing Text Messages and Fake Sites
description: Recognize delivery, unpaid-bill and account-suspension scams, and respond safely after opening a suspicious link.
text: How to Recognize Phishing Text Messages and Fake Sites Recognize delivery, unpaid-bill and account-suspension scams, and respond safely after opening a suspicious link. Do not sign in or pay through an SMS link A sender name alone does not prove authenticity Be cautious of urgency, shortened URLs and attached apps Do not tap the message link; check the claim through the official app or a bookmark you created yourself. Read the full domain and look for character substitutions, misleading subdomains and shortened URLs. Do not enter passwords, card data or one-time codes requested by the message. If you only opened the page, close it and check that nothing was downloaded and no profile was installed. If you entered credentials, change the password immediately from the official site, update reused passwords, and review sessions and two-step verification. If you entered card data, contact the card issuer; if you installed an app, disconnect and seek official support. Report the message as spam and preserve only the evidence needed.

title: Phone Screen Is Black but the Phone Is On: Safe Troubleshooting Steps
description: Use sound, vibration, charging, forced restart, and damage checks to diagnose a phone that appears powered on while the display stays black.
text: Phone Screen Is Black but the Phone Is On: Safe Troubleshooting Steps Use sound, vibration, charging, forced restart, and damage checks to diagnose a phone that appears powered on while the display stays black. Display and screen Check whether calls, vibration, alarms, or notification sounds still work Remove a case or protector that may obstruct buttons or sensors Note whether the issue began after a drop, liquid exposure, or extreme temperature

title: Touchscreen Not Responding or Moving by Itself: What to Check
description: Troubleshoot dead zones and ghost touches by checking moisture, protectors, chargers, apps, restart behavior, and physical damage.
text: Touchscreen Not Responding or Moving by Itself: What to Check Troubleshoot dead zones and ghost touches by checking moisture, protectors, chargers, apps, restart behavior, and physical damage. Display and screen Make sure the screen and your fingers are clean and dry Temporarily remove the case and screen protector Check whether the problem occurs only while charging

title: Phone Screen Flickering, Lines, or Strange Colors: Diagnostic Guide
description: Separate display settings, app rendering, heat, and hardware faults when a phone flickers or shows persistent lines or abnormal colors.
text: Phone Screen Flickering, Lines, or Strange Colors: Diagnostic Guide Separate display settings, app rendering, heat, and hardware faults when a phone flickers or shows persistent lines or abnormal colors. Display and screen Take a screenshot and see whether the defect appears in the saved image Recall recent brightness, color, accessibility, or developer-setting changes Note any recent drop, pressure, overheating, or liquid exposure

title: Phone Screen Will Not Auto-Rotate: Settings and Sensor Checks
description: Check rotation lock, app support, display controls, and orientation sensors when a phone remains in portrait mode.
text: Phone Screen Will Not Auto-Rotate: Settings and Sensor Checks Check rotation lock, app support, display controls, and orientation sensors when a phone remains in portrait mode. Display and screen Confirm that portrait orientation lock or rotation lock is off Test in a photo or video app that supports landscape orientation Hold and rotate the phone rather than leaving it flat on a table

title: No Sound from a Phone: Separate Ringtone, Media, Call, and Speaker Issues
description: Identify which audio path is silent, then check volume controls, mute modes, Bluetooth output, apps, and speaker condition.
text: No Sound from a Phone: Separate Ringtone, Media, Call, and Speaker Issues Identify which audio path is silent, then check volume controls, mute modes, Bluetooth output, apps, and speaker condition. Sound and microphone Determine whether the missing sound affects calls, media, alarms, notifications, or everything Press the volume keys and confirm which volume channel is changing Check whether audio is being routed to Bluetooth earbuds, a car, or another device

title: Phone Microphone Not Working or Others Cannot Hear You: Troubleshooting Guide
description: Compare calls, recordings, videos, and individual apps to isolate permissions, accessories, blocked openings, or microphone hardware faults.
text: Phone Microphone Not Working or Others Cannot Hear You: Troubleshooting Guide Compare calls, recordings, videos, and individual apps to isolate permissions, accessories, blocked openings, or microphone hardware faults. Sound and microphone Record and play back your voice with the built-in recorder Compare a normal call, speakerphone, front-camera video, and rear-camera video Remove any case or protector that may cover a microphone opening

title: Phone Camera Will Not Open, Shows Black, or Will Not Focus: Fixes to Try
description: Troubleshoot a black camera preview, app launch failure, or poor focus by checking the lens, permissions, app conflicts, storage, and hardware.
text: Phone Camera Will Not Open, Shows Black, or Will Not Focus: Fixes to Try Troubleshoot a black camera preview, app launch failure, or poor focus by checking the lens, permissions, app conflicts, storage, and hardware. Camera and photos Compare the built-in Camera app with another trusted app that uses the camera Switch between front and rear cameras Inspect lens dirt, the case, lens protectors, and magnetic accessories

title: Phone Notifications Not Arriving or Delayed: Complete Checklist
description: Separate one-app notification issues from system-wide delays by checking permissions, Focus modes, battery restrictions, connectivity, and account settings.
text: Phone Notifications Not Arriving or Delayed: Complete Checklist Separate one-app notification issues from system-wide delays by checking permissions, Focus modes, battery restrictions, connectivity, and account settings. Notifications and alarms Determine whether one app or all apps are affected Review Focus, Do Not Disturb, silent, and scheduled modes Check whether notifications arrive in a batch only after opening the app

title: Phone Alarm Did Not Ring or Was Too Quiet: What to Check
description: Verify time, repeat days, alarm sound, volume, Bluetooth output, clock-app restrictions, and a real test before relying on an alarm.
text: Phone Alarm Did Not Ring or Was Too Quiet: What to Check Verify time, repeat days, alarm sound, volume, Bluetooth output, clock-app restrictions, and a real test before relying on an alarm. Notifications and alarms Confirm AM or PM and the selected repeat days Make sure the alarm sound is not set to None or silent Create a test alarm one or two minutes ahead and listen for it

title: Phone Keyboard Does Not Appear or You Cannot Type: Fixes to Try
description: Check the app, external keyboard connection, active input method, language settings, and keyboard app when the on-screen keyboard will not open.
text: Phone Keyboard Does Not Appear or You Cannot Type: Fixes to Try Check the app, external keyboard connection, active input method, language settings, and keyboard app when the on-screen keyboard will not open. Keyboard and text input Test a text field in another app Check for a connected Bluetooth or USB keyboard Make sure voice or handwriting input has not replaced the normal keyboard

title: Phone Cannot Make Calls: Network, SIM, and Account Checks
description: Troubleshoot outgoing calls that fail, disconnect immediately, or show emergency calls only by checking coverage, SIM selection, restrictions, and carrier status.
text: Phone Cannot Make Calls: Network, SIM, and Account Checks Troubleshoot outgoing calls that fail, disconnect immediately, or show emergency calls only by checking coverage, SIM selection, restrictions, and carrier status. Calls and messages Try more than one valid phone number Look for No service, Emergency calls only, or Airplane mode indicators Check carrier billing, suspension, roaming, and plan conditions

title: Phone Not Receiving Calls: Why Calls Go Straight to Voicemail
description: Check signal, Focus modes, blocked callers, forwarding, dual-SIM settings, and carrier provisioning when incoming calls never ring.
text: Phone Not Receiving Calls: Why Calls Go Straight to Voicemail Check signal, Focus modes, blocked callers, forwarding, dual-SIM settings, and carrier provisioning when incoming calls never ring. Calls and messages Determine whether all callers or only certain numbers are affected Review Focus, silent mode, and silence-unknown-callers settings Place a real test call from another phone

title: SMS or Verification Code Not Received: Troubleshooting Checklist
description: Separate a general SMS reception problem from a missing one-time code by checking line registration, blocks, spam filtering, number format, and sender limits.
text: SMS or Verification Code Not Received: Troubleshooting Checklist Separate a general SMS reception problem from a missing one-time code by checking line registration, blocks, spam filtering, number format, and sender limits. Calls and messages Determine whether ordinary SMS, verification codes, or both are missing Verify country code, leading zero, and the registered phone number Check spam, blocked senders, filtered tabs, and archived messages

title: Phone Hotspot or Tethering Not Working: Connection Checklist
description: Fix a hotspot that is invisible, rejects the password, or connects without internet by checking mobile data, plan support, frequency, power limits, and carrier settings.
text: Phone Hotspot or Tethering Not Working: Connection Checklist Fix a hotspot that is invisible, rejects the password, or connects without internet by checking mobile data, plan support, frequency, power limits, and carrier settings. Connectivity Confirm the phone itself can use mobile data Verify that the carrier plan permits tethering Check connected-device limits, battery level, and data allowance

title: Phone Says No SIM or Invalid SIM: Safe Checks and Fixes
description: Troubleshoot No SIM, Invalid SIM, or emergency-calls-only messages by checking line state, SIM seating, device lock, compatibility, and carrier provisioning.
text: Phone Says No SIM or Invalid SIM: Safe Checks and Fixes Troubleshoot No SIM, Invalid SIM, or emergency-calls-only messages by checking line state, SIM seating, device lock, compatibility, and carrier provisioning. Connectivity Record the exact message: No SIM, Invalid SIM, No service, or Emergency calls only Note whether the SIM worked before or the problem began after changing phones Check whether the phone is locked to a different carrier

title: eSIM Will Not Activate or Add: QR Code and Transfer Checklist
description: Resolve eSIM QR-code, pending-activation, and missing-line problems by checking device support, Wi-Fi, one-time codes, transfer rules, and carrier provisioning.
text: eSIM Will Not Activate or Add: QR Code and Transfer Checklist Resolve eSIM QR-code, pending-activation, and missing-line problems by checking device support, Wi-Fi, one-time codes, transfer rules, and carrier provisioning. Connectivity Confirm that both the device and carrier support eSIM Connect to stable Wi-Fi Check whether the QR code is single-use or already installed on the old phone

title: Phone GPS or Location Is Wrong or Unavailable: Troubleshooting Guide
description: Fix inaccurate or unavailable location by checking app permissions, precision options, network assistance, environment, compass behavior, and sensors.
text: Phone GPS or Location Is Wrong or Unavailable: Troubleshooting Guide Fix inaccurate or unavailable location by checking app permissions, precision options, network assistance, environment, compass behavior, and sensors. Connectivity Test outdoors with a clear view of the sky Compare the built-in map with another trusted location app Confirm the affected app has location permission

title: Computer Does Not Recognize a Phone over USB: Data Connection Fixes
description: When a phone charges but files do not appear, check the data cable, unlocked state, USB mode, trust prompt, computer software, and port condition.
text: Computer Does Not Recognize a Phone over USB: Data Connection Fixes When a phone charges but files do not appear, check the data cable, unlocked state, USB mode, trust prompt, computer software, and port condition. Connectivity Confirm the cable supports data and is not charge-only Unlock the phone before and during connection Compare another cable, USB port, and computer

title: NFC or Contactless Payment Not Working on a Phone: Checklist
description: Troubleshoot tap-to-pay and NFC reading by checking support, default wallet, authentication, case interference, card status, terminals, and service outages.
text: NFC or Contactless Payment Not Working on a Phone: Checklist Troubleshoot tap-to-pay and NFC reading by checking support, default wallet, authentication, case interference, card status, terminals, and service outages. Connectivity Confirm that the phone, region, bank, and service support NFC payments Check card expiry, suspension, and identity-verification status Remove cases or card storage that may interfere with the NFC antenna

title: Phone Overheating or Temperature Warning: Safe Cooling Steps
description: Distinguish expected heat during charging, gaming, navigation, or updates from battery danger signs and cool the device without causing condensation or damage.
text: Phone Overheating or Temperature Warning: Safe Cooling Steps Distinguish expected heat during charging, gaming, navigation, or updates from battery danger signs and cool the device without causing condensation or damage. Power and temperature Note whether heat followed gaming, video, navigation, charging, updates, or direct sunlight Check for a temperature warning, swelling, odor, smoke, or leakage See whether one app consistently causes the heat

title: Phone Charging Slowly: Find the Bottleneck and Improve Speed
description: Diagnose slow charging by comparing adapter output, cable condition, port contact, temperature, active power use, and optimized-charging limits.
text: Phone Charging Slowly: Find the Bottleneck and Improve Speed Diagnose slow charging by comparing adapter output, cable condition, port contact, temperature, active power use, and optimized-charging limits. Power and temperature Confirm that the adapter and cable support the phone’s charging standard Stop gaming, navigation, video, hotspot, or other heavy use while charging Check whether the phone is unusually hot or cold

title: Wireless Charging Not Working or Stops: Alignment and Safety Checks
description: Check device support, coil alignment, cases, foreign objects, power supply, temperature, and charger condition when wireless charging fails.
text: Wireless Charging Not Working or Stops: Alignment and Safety Checks Check device support, coil alignment, cases, foreign objects, power supply, temperature, and charger condition when wireless charging fails. Power and temperature Confirm the phone and charger support a compatible wireless standard Remove metal plates, magnets, cards, and thick cases Verify that wired charging works normally

title: Phone Dropped in Water: Immediate Safety and Recovery Steps
description: Reduce short-circuit, corrosion, and battery risks after liquid exposure by handling power, charging, drying, backup, and repair in the correct order.
text: Phone Dropped in Water: Immediate Safety and Recovery Steps Reduce short-circuit, corrosion, and battery risks after liquid exposure by handling power, charging, drying, backup, and repair in the correct order. Power and temperature Identify whether the liquid was fresh water, salt water, a drink, or cleaning fluid Check for heat, swelling, odor, smoke, or leakage Review the manufacturer water-resistance conditions and warranty limitations

title: Phone Software Update Failed: Download and Install Fixes
description: Resolve update-check, download, verification, and installation errors by checking compatibility, battery, storage, Wi-Fi, server status, and recovery options.
text: Phone Software Update Failed: Download and Install Fixes Resolve update-check, download, verification, and installation errors by checking compatibility, battery, storage, Wi-Fi, server status, and recovery options. Software updates Confirm the phone model is eligible for the update Verify adequate free storage and battery charge Prepare stable Wi-Fi and external power

title: Phone Update Stuck on Progress Bar or Rebooting: Safe Recovery Steps
description: Decide when to wait, keep charging, force-restart, or use official recovery when a software update appears frozen or repeatedly restarts.
text: Phone Update Stuck on Progress Bar or Rebooting: Safe Recovery Steps Decide when to wait, keep charging, force-restart, or use official recovery when a software update appears frozen or repeatedly restarts. Software updates Record how long the update has run and whether the progress indicator changes Keep the phone connected to a stable power source Check for abnormal heat, swelling, odor, smoke, or other battery danger signs

title: iPhone Stuck on the Apple Logo: Data-Safe Recovery Order
description: Work through waiting, one forced restart, and Recovery mode when an iPhone remains on the Apple logo or progress bar, prioritizing Update before an erase.
text: iPhone Stuck on the Apple Logo: Data-Safe Recovery Order Work through waiting, one forced restart, and Recovery mode when an iPhone remains on the Apple logo or progress bar, prioritizing Update before an erase. Startup and recovery Record whether a progress bar has moved during the last hour Note whether the issue followed an update, restore, or data transfer Confirm the last backup date and Apple Account recovery access

title: Android Phone Stuck in a Boot Loop or on the Logo: Recovery Guide
description: Troubleshoot repeated reboots and logo freezes through charging, button checks, one forced restart, Safe mode, and model-specific official recovery.
text: Android Phone Stuck in a Boot Loop or on the Logo: Recovery Guide Troubleshoot repeated reboots and logo freezes through charging, button checks, one forced restart, Safe mode, and model-specific official recovery. Startup and recovery Remove a case that may hold the power or volume buttons down Recall recent apps, system updates, drops, or liquid exposure Check the last Google backup and photo-sync status

title: How to Use iPhone Recovery Mode: Update vs Restore Explained
description: Prepare a computer, enter Recovery mode, and understand the data-preserving Update option versus the data-erasing Restore option.
text: How to Use iPhone Recovery Mode: Update vs Restore Explained Prepare a computer, enter Recovery mode, and understand the data-preserving Update option versus the data-erasing Restore option. Startup and recovery Prepare a compatible Mac or Windows computer and reliable cable Update Finder, Apple Devices, or iTunes Confirm backups, Apple Account access, and two-factor recovery methods

title: Use Android Safe Mode to Identify a Problem App
description: Test whether restarts, freezing, battery drain, pop-ups, or other issues are caused by a downloaded app, then remove candidates one at a time.
text: Use Android Safe Mode to Identify a Problem App Test whether restarts, freezing, battery drain, pop-ups, or other issues are caused by a downloaded app, then remove candidates one at a time. Startup and recovery Recall whether the issue began after installing or updating an app Confirm account access for critical apps before removing anything Find the manufacturer’s exact Safe mode start and exit procedure for the model

title: How to Review Phone App Permissions and Choose Safely
description: Limit camera, microphone, location, photos, contacts, Bluetooth, and notification access to what each app genuinely needs.
text: How to Review Phone App Permissions and Choose Safely Limit camera, microphone, location, photos, contacts, Bluetooth, and notification access to what each app genuinely needs. Security and permissions Ask whether the permission is essential to the app’s main function Compare Always, While Using, Ask Every Time, and One-Time options Review the developer, update history, and privacy explanation

title: How to Check and Remove a Suspicious App Safely
description: Handle an unknown app, sudden ads, abnormal battery use, or excessive permissions by preserving evidence, protecting accounts, revoking control, and uninstalling safely.
text: How to Check and Remove a Suspicious App Safely Handle an unknown app, sudden ads, abnormal battery use, or excessive permissions by preserving evidence, protecting accounts, revoking control, and uninstalling safely. Security and permissions Record the app name, icon, source, developer, and install date Use another trusted device to check bank, email, and social account login history Check for device-admin, accessibility, VPN, profile, or management privileges

title: Phone Browser Pop-Ups, Fake Warnings, or Redirects Will Not Stop
description: Remove persistent scam warnings and redirects by closing tabs safely, reviewing site notifications and data, checking profiles or apps, and protecting exposed accounts.
text: Phone Browser Pop-Ups, Fake Warnings, or Redirects Will Not Stop Remove persistent scam warnings and redirects by closing tabs safely, reviewing site notifications and data, checking profiles or apps, and protecting exposed accounts. Security and permissions Determine whether the warning appears only in the browser or across the phone Recall whether you tapped a phone number, install button, calendar, or profile prompt Review websites allowed to send browser notifications

title: Transfer Data from an Old iPhone to a New iPhone with Quick Start
description: Compare Quick Start, iCloud, and encrypted computer backups and verify eSIM, authentication, messaging, Wallet, and important data before erasing the old iPhone.
text: Transfer Data from an Old iPhone to a New iPhone with Quick Start Compare Quick Start, iCloud, and encrypted computer backups and verify eSIM, authentication, messaging, Wallet, and important data before erasing the old iPhone. Data transfer Confirm the Apple Account, old-device passcode, and a current backup Charge both iPhones and enable Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Review separate transfer requirements for eSIM, authenticators, banking, and messaging apps

title: Transfer Data from Android to a New Android Phone
description: Use Google backup, cable transfer, wireless copy, and manufacturer tools while separately preparing authenticator, eSIM, banking, and messaging data.
text: Transfer Data from Android to a New Android Phone Use Google backup, cable transfer, wireless copy, and manufacturer tools while separately preparing authenticator, eSIM, banking, and messaging data. Data transfer Confirm Google Account access, screen lock, and the latest backup time Charge both phones and prepare a supported USB cable or adapter Decide whether to use Google setup, an official manufacturer tool, or both

title: Transfer from iPhone to Android: Photos, Contacts, Messages, and More
description: Use the official Android switch process while preparing iMessage, FaceTime, eSIM, authenticators, WhatsApp, and data that may not transfer automatically.
text: Transfer from iPhone to Android: Photos, Contacts, Messages, and More Use the official Android switch process while preparing iMessage, FaceTime, eSIM, authenticators, WhatsApp, and data that may not transfer automatically. Data transfer Confirm access to both Apple Account and Google Account Prepare a cable and adapter compatible with the new Android phone Review iMessage, FaceTime, eSIM, and authenticator transfer steps

title: Transfer from Android to iPhone with Move to iOS
description: Use Apple’s official Move to iOS process for contacts, messages, photos, and supported apps, then handle eSIM, authentication, and messaging services separately.
text: Transfer from Android to iPhone with Move to iOS Use Apple’s official Move to iOS process for contacts, messages, photos, and supported apps, then handle eSIM, authentication, and messaging services separately. Data transfer Charge Android and the new iPhone and prepare stable Wi-Fi Confirm that the iPhone has enough storage for selected Android data Review separate requirements for authenticators, messaging, eSIM, and banking apps

title: Android Private DNS Not Working: Advanced Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose Private DNS hostnames that will not save, break connectivity, or fail only on certain Wi-Fi networks by separating provider, VPN, certificate, and network restrictions.

title: Phone APN Settings Not Saving or Working
description: Fix APN profiles that do not save or provide data even when 4G or 5G appears by checking APN type, authentication, MCC/MNC, and carrier provisioning.

title: Why 5G Is Not Showing on a 5G Phone
description: Diagnose a 5G-capable phone that stays on LTE by checking bands, NSA versus SA, power settings, SIM provisioning, and local coverage.

title: Mobile Data Drops During Calls: VoLTE Checks
description: When mobile data slows or stops during calls, check VoLTE, IMS registration, the selected voice line, and carrier support.

title: Wi-Fi Calling Not Activating or Completing Calls
description: Troubleshoot missing Wi-Fi Calling controls, activation failures, and calls that will not complete by checking plan eligibility, emergency address, router behavior, and VPNs.

title: Public Wi-Fi Login Page Not Opening
description: Open a captive portal that does not appear on hotel, airport, or cafe Wi-Fi by checking HTTPS redirection, Private DNS, VPN, randomized MAC, and expired sessions.

title: Randomized MAC Address Causing Wi-Fi Problems
description: Resolve Wi-Fi registration failures caused by randomized MAC or private addresses while preserving privacy where fixed device registration is not required.

title: Dual-SIM Phone Uses the Wrong Line
description: Stop a dual-SIM phone from using the wrong line for calls, texts, or data by checking defaults, per-contact choices, automatic switching, and roaming.

title: Roaming Shows Signal but Mobile Data Does Not Work
description: When roaming shows signal but no data, verify data roaming, partner networks, APN, spending limits, and manual network selection.

title: VPN Connected but No Internet on a Phone
description: Diagnose a phone VPN that reports connected but has no internet by checking DNS, always-on VPN, kill switch, MTU, protocol, and competing VPN profiles.

title: RCS Chat Features Stuck on Verifying Your Number
description: Fix RCS that remains on verifying or falls back to SMS by checking the number, default SIM, carrier services, VPN, and registration on an old phone.

title: Android Notification History: Enable, Read, and Troubleshoot It
description: Use Android Notification History and understand activation requirements, retention limits, excluded notifications, and manufacturer differences.

title: Android Notification Category or Channel Is Missing
description: Find missing Android notification categories for messages, calls, sync, and conversations by checking channel creation, app version, and conversation controls.

title: Notifications Arrive Late When an App Is Closed
description: Fix notifications that arrive only after opening an app by checking background data, power saving, auto-start, data saver, and push connectivity.

title: Android Battery Optimization Stops an App
description: When tracking, VPN, health, or sync apps stop, understand battery-optimization exclusions and what to verify before allowing unrestricted background use.

title: Links or Files Open in the Wrong Default App
description: Fix web links, PDFs, maps, or phone numbers opening in the wrong app by checking defaults, supported links, share actions, and file associations.

title: Android App Links Open in a Browser Instead of the App
description: When verified URLs open in a browser instead of the app, inspect supported-link settings, domain verification, defaults, and competing apps.

title: Picture-in-Picture Is Not Working on a Phone
description: Fix video that does not enter picture-in-picture by checking app permission, subscription eligibility, exit gesture, power mode, and content restrictions.

title: Phone Screen Recording Has No Audio
description: Fix silent screen recordings or recordings with only microphone or device audio by checking the audio source, microphone permission, protected calls, and Bluetooth routing.

title: Phone Clipboard Copy and Paste Not Working
description: Fix copied text that disappears or cannot be pasted by checking clipboard history, sensitive-data protection, keyboard behavior, and work-profile boundaries.

title: iPhone Focus Mode Is Blocking Important Notifications
description: Fix important alerts blocked by iPhone Focus by checking shared Focus state, schedules, filters, allowed people and apps, and emergency exceptions.

title: iCloud Private Relay Causes Website or Wi-Fi Problems
description: When Private Relay breaks a site, changes location, or triggers a network warning, check IP tracking controls, network compatibility, and VPN overlap.

title: Why iPhone Stolen Device Protection Adds a Security Delay
description: Understand the security delay for Apple Account or passcode changes, including familiar locations, biometric checks, and protected actions.

title: iPhone System Data Storage Is Very Large
description: Investigate iPhone System Data growth by separating caches, logs, streaming data, update files, and incomplete sync operations.

title: iPhone iCloud Backup Is Stuck or Calculating Time
description: Fix an iCloud backup stuck on calculating or not progressing by checking storage, Wi-Fi quality, power mode, included apps, and encryption.

title: iPhone App Shows a Cloud Icon and Will Not Open
description: Understand offloaded apps with cloud icons, automatic offloading, App Store availability, and which local documents remain.

title: iPhone Says This Accessory May Not Be Supported
description: Resolve iPhone accessory-not-supported warnings by checking certification, debris, power requirements, liquid detection, and iOS compatibility.

title: iPhone Unavailable or Security Lockout: What to Do
description: Handle iPhone Unavailable or Security Lockout safely by understanding wait times, previous passcode options, device erase, and Apple Account requirements.

title: iPhone Background App Refresh Is Not Working
description: Fix Background App Refresh that is disabled or ineffective by checking Low Power Mode, cellular access, Screen Time, and force-closing habits.

title: AirDrop Is Stuck on Waiting
description: Fix AirDrop stuck on waiting or unable to find the recipient by checking receiving mode, contact matching, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, and Personal Hotspot.

title: Android System WebView Causes Apps to Crash
description: When multiple apps crash or embedded web pages turn blank, inspect Android System WebView, Chrome updates, beta builds, and cached components.

title: Android Work Profile Is Paused or Not Syncing
description: Fix grayed-out work apps or stopped notifications by checking the Work Profile toggle, schedules, management policy, and device-policy controller connectivity.

title: Google Play Protect Blocks or Disables an App
description: When Play Protect blocks or disables an app, verify its source, signature, update state, and management policy without casually disabling protection.

title: Pixel Adaptive Connectivity and Unexpected 5G or Wi-Fi Switching
description: Understand how Pixel Adaptive Connectivity affects 5G use, battery consumption, and network switching, and how to run a controlled disabled test.

title: How to Use Pixel Device Diagnostics and Battery Checks
description: Use Pixel settings and official support diagnostics to check battery, connectivity, and sensors and collect useful information before repair.

title: Pixel Repair Mode: Preparation and Limitations
description: Prepare for Pixel Repair Mode by understanding eligibility, backups, screen-lock requirements, what technicians can access, and how to exit.

title: Samsung Galaxy Maintenance Mode: Setup and Repair Checklist
description: Use Samsung Galaxy Maintenance Mode before repair, including eligibility, backup, system-log options, technician access, and exit steps.

title: Galaxy Secure Folder Notifications Are Missing or Hidden
description: Fix missing or hidden Secure Folder notifications by checking folder lock state, content visibility, power controls, and differences from a work profile.

title: How to Check Android Device Administrators and Management Profiles
description: When settings or app removal are blocked, distinguish device administrators, work profiles, VPN or certificates, and parental controls.

title: Android Date or Time Errors Cause Certificate Warnings
description: Fix certificate-expiry warnings caused by incorrect Android date or time by checking automatic time, time zone, SIM source, clock retention, and root certificates.

title: HEIC or HEIF Photos Will Not Open or Share
description: When phone photos will not open on another computer or app, check HEIF support, conversion, automatic sharing compatibility, and quality preservation.

title: Why HDR Photos Change Color on Other Devices or Social Apps
description: Explain washed-out, dark, or oversaturated HDR photos by checking HDR display support, color space, tone mapping, and social-platform conversion.

title: Phone Video Is Too Large to Send
description: Send large 4K or HDR phone videos by comparing resolution, frame rate, codec, trimming, compression, and cloud-link sharing.

title: Phone USB-C Video Output Is Not Working
description: When USB-C will not output video, verify DisplayPort Alt Mode, device support, cable capability, hub power, and desktop-mode requirements.

title: Phone Does Not Recognize an External Microphone
description: Fix USB-C, Lightning, or 3.5 mm microphones that fall back to the built-in mic by checking TRRS standard, power, adapters, and app input selection.

title: Android USB OTG Storage Is Not Mounting
description: When Android does not mount a USB drive or card reader, check OTG support, power, file system, USB mode, and encryption.

title: Quick Share or Nearby Sharing Cannot Find the Other Device
description: Fix nearby transfer discovery or stalled transfers by checking visibility, account matching, Bluetooth and Wi-Fi, screen lock, and file limits.

title: Apple CarPlay Keeps Disconnecting
description: Fix wired or wireless CarPlay disconnects by checking the cable, vehicle USB port, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, VPN, and head-unit firmware.

title: Android Auto Keeps Disconnecting or Will Not Start
description: Fix Android Auto that disconnects or will not launch by checking USB quality, wireless requirements, permissions, battery controls, and head-unit compatibility.

title: Bluetooth Audio Stutters or Has High Latency
description: Fix Bluetooth audio stutter or latency by checking distance, 2.4 GHz interference, multipoint, codec negotiation, battery, and developer overrides.

title: Phone Passkey Sign-In Fails
description: Fix missing passkeys or failed cross-device QR sign-in by checking the credential provider, screen lock, Bluetooth, account sync, and website domain.

title: Transfer an Authenticator App Before Resetting a Phone
description: Avoid losing two-factor codes by checking cloud sync, export options, backup codes, and per-service re-enrollment before resetting a phone.

title: Why Biometrics Are Unavailable After a Phone Restart
description: Understand why biometrics require a passcode after restart, extended inactivity, or security changes, and use legitimate recovery methods if the passcode is forgotten.

title: Camera or Microphone Privacy Indicator Stays On
description: When a camera or microphone privacy indicator remains visible, identify the active app, background permission, call service, accessibility feature, or system process.

title: Unknown Certificate or Unsafe Connection Warning on a Phone
description: When a certificate warning appears, distinguish clock errors, captive portals, VPN inspection, user certificates, and fraudulent sites before deciding whether to continue.

title: Find My Device Shows the Phone Offline
description: Understand an offline lost phone by checking power, connectivity, location, offline-finding networks, last known location, and queued remote actions.

title: Emergency SOS Triggers Accidentally on a Phone
description: When button presses trigger Emergency SOS, review the activation gesture, countdown, emergency contacts, regional behavior, and safe cancellation.

title: Phone Repair Privacy and Security Checklist
description: Prepare a phone for repair by handling backups, repair mode, SIM, payments, accounts, screen lock, and device finding in a safe order.

title: App Tracking Permission Is Missing or Cannot Be Changed
description: When app tracking permission is missing or disabled, check age, managed accounts, profiles, and restrictions after recent account or device changes.

title: Public Wi-Fi Certificate Warning: Should You Continue?
description: Evaluate certificate warnings on hotel or airport Wi-Fi by checking the captive portal, evil-twin risk, clock accuracy, VPN behavior, and whether sensitive activity should wait.

title: Advanced Network and Carrier Settings
description: Diagnose APN, Private DNS, VoLTE, Wi-Fi calling, dual-SIM, VPN, and other network settings that basic restarts do not resolve.

title: Notifications and App Behavior
description: Troubleshoot notification channels, background restrictions, default apps, app links, screen recording, and other app-level behavior.

title: Advanced iPhone Settings and Recovery
description: Work through Focus, Private Relay, System Data, iCloud backup, security lockout, AirDrop, and other iPhone-specific issues.

title: Android, Pixel, and Galaxy Device Care
description: Understand WebView, work profiles, Play Protect, Pixel diagnostics and repair mode, Galaxy Maintenance Mode, and related device-care features.

title: Media, USB, Audio, and In-Car Connections
description: Troubleshoot HEIF, HDR, video size, USB-C display output, external microphones, OTG, CarPlay, Android Auto, and Bluetooth audio.

title: Security, Authentication, and Repair Privacy
description: Handle passkeys, authenticator transfers, biometrics, certificate warnings, device finding, repair privacy, and security indicators.
